title: supera per-repo config
description: Lives at <repo>/.claude/supera.json. Tells supera's ship/pr-watch/auditors how to operate in THIS repo. Written by /supera-init; safe to hand-edit.

stack.description: Primary toolchain, used to pick audit/verify behaviour.

verify.description: Commands the engineer runs to self-verify before returning, and pr-watch uses to reproduce CI failures. Any field may be omitted if the stack has no such step.

build.description: Build / typecheck, e.g. 'pnpm turbo run build'.

test.description: Unit tests, e.g. 'pnpm turbo run test:unit'.

worktree.description: How ship creates its isolated workspace.

dir.description: Directory (relative to repo root) holding feature worktrees.

base.description: Branch new work is cut from.

postCreate.description: Command run inside a fresh worktree, usually the install step. Defaults to verify.install if omitted.

clickup.description: ClickUp wiring. Set to null (or omit) to run ticket-less: ship/pr-watch skip all ClickUp steps and operate purely on git + GitHub.

listId.description: List that holds this repo's backlog. Required for ticket creation.

projectTag.description: Constant ClickUp tag applied to every ticket from this repo (the project name). Lets a shared board be filtered per-repo. Must pre-exist in the space (best-effort). Omit/null to skip tagging.

statuses.description: Maps supera lifecycle phases to THIS repo's ClickUp status names (statuses are space-specific). Each skill reads CONFIG.clickup.statuses.<key>; omit a key to keep its default. Used only when clickup.listId is set. The human-only verdicts (completed/accepted) are intentionally absent — supera never sets them.

ready.description: Refined and ready to ship. Set by /refine-ticket.

review.description: PR open: CI + human review. Set by /ship at PR push.

blocked.description: Stuck: repeated CI failure or unresolved blocker. Set by /pr-watch.

rejected.description: PR closed without merge. Terminal. Set by /pr-watch.

closed.description: Merged, done, archived. Set by /ship on merge.

audits.description: Optional audit agents ship/CI can run.

freshness.description: Enable supera-freshness-auditor (dependency CURRENCY, not security). Disjoint from audits.supplyChain: no CVE/secret/override logic.

level.description: off = report-only (recommend/flag, never auto). patch = auto-apply in-range PATCH bumps; in-range minors are recommended. minor = also auto-apply CLEAN in-range MINOR bumps (breaking-flavored or notes-unreachable minors downgrade to recommend); majors always FLAG. Range-widening => FLAG at every level.

minReleaseAgeDays.description: Cooldown: only auto-apply a version whose exact-resolved publish date is at least this many days old (defuses day-zero compromised/yanked releases). If the publish date is unreachable (e.g. crates.io REST down) => UNVERIFIED => FLAG, never auto.

ci.description: CI runtime and the native package-manager audit commands the supera audit workflow runs in CI. Schema-first per the roadmap; the Phase 1 audit workflow reads these.

provider.description: CI provider whose workflow files supera emits. Currently only GitHub Actions is supported.

audit.description: Native audit command per package manager. The audit workflow picks the one matching the detected stack; omit a manager the repo doesn't use.

automation.description: Which triggers enable supera's CI automation workflows. Schema-first; the Phase 1 audit workflow is emitted/gated from these flags.

audit.description: Triggers for the supera supply-chain audit workflow. All default off so the workflow is inert until a repo opts in.

schedule.description: Run the audit on a weekly cron schedule.

label.description: Run the audit when a PR is labeled 'supera:audit'.

prWatch.description: Triggers for the supera pr-watch workflow (drives an open PR to green headless). All default off so the emitted workflow is inert until a repo opts in.

checkSuite.description: Run pr-watch when a check_suite completes.

ship.description: Triggers for the supera ship workflow (headless /ship from a labeled issue). Off by default so the emitted workflow is inert until a repo opts in.

issueLabel.description: Run /ship when an issue is labeled 'supera:ship'.

review.description: Pre-merge review rigor for /pr-watch. CI remains the quality gate; this only adds review-judgment layers on top.

consensus.description: Merge-readiness vote run once per /pr-watch cycle, after code-review / test-hygiene / audit fixes are addressed. N independent reviewer agents each adversarially hunt for ONE blocking defect; the PR is declared ready only when at least `quorum` of them clear it. Reviewers judge only — any blocking finding is delegated to supera-engineer, never edited by the reviewer.

voters.description: Independent reviewer agents that vote on merge-readiness. 1 (default) disables the gate — single review pass, the original behaviour.

quorum.description: APPROVE votes required to declare the PR ready. Defaults to a majority of `voters` (floor(voters/2)+1) when omitted.

lenses.description: Extra specialist review lenses fanned out in parallel (via the Workflow primitive) during /pr-watch step 6a, on top of the base code-review pass — each posts findings as PR comments for more signal per cycle. Empty (default) keeps the single code-review pass. These run on the per-PR hot path, so the set is bounded to the listed specialists; keep the list short.

security.description: Guard rails the engineer and /pr-watch enforce on every change.

denyPaths.description: Globs the engineer must never create, modify, or stage, and that /pr-watch refuses to let into a PR (secrets, private keys). A changed file matching any glob is a hard merge blocker surfaced to the user. Set to [] to disable. Omit to use the secret/key defaults.
